title: Why Traceability Matters
content: Without traceability: unknown scope; manual change impact checking; uncertain test coverage; orphaned code. With traceability: know exactly what is affected by any change; prove all requirements are implemented/tested; verify scope; show provenance of every feature; find gaps orphans and gold-plating.
tags: traceability benefits scope coverage impact orphan gold-plating audit

title: Forward Traceability
content: Forward traceability traces from requirements downstream to implementation and tests. Direction: Requirement → Design → Code → Test Cases. Answers: "Where is this requirement implemented?" and "How is this requirement tested?" Ensures all requirements are built and verified. Detects unimplemented requirements.
tags: forward-traceability downstream implementation test-cases requirement coverage

title: Backward Traceability
content: Backward traceability traces from implementation artifacts upstream to requirements and business goals. Direction: Code → Design → Requirement → Business Need → Business Goal. Answers: "Why does this exist?" and "What business need justifies this code?" Detects gold-plating (code without requirements).
tags: backward-traceability upstream business-goal justification gold-plating source

title: Bidirectional Traceability
content: Bidirectional traceability maintains links in both forward and backward directions simultaneously. Enables full impact analysis when any artifact changes. Required for: regulatory compliance (FDA IEC62304 DO-178C); large complex systems; formal change control. RTM supports bidirectional tracing.
tags: bidirectional-traceability both-directions RTM impact regulatory compliance full-chain

title: Traceability Types Matrix
content: 5 traceability types: Backward to Source (FR←SR←BR "why does this exist"); Forward to Implementation (FR→Design→Code "where is this built"); Forward to Test (FR→Test Cases "how is this tested"); Vertical between levels (Business→Stakeholder→Solution); Horizontal between peers (FR↔UC↔UI Screen).
tags: traceability-types matrix backward forward vertical horizontal peer

title: RTM Template Structure
content: RTM columns: REQ ID; Requirement Description; Priority (MoSCoW); Source (BR/SR reference); Design Ref; Code Module; Test Cases; Status; Owner; Release. Coverage dashboard shows: Backward Trace %; Design Coverage %; Implementation Coverage %; Test Coverage %. Status summary: Verified/In-Progress/Pending/Deferred/Rejected counts.
tags: RTM template columns coverage dashboard status requirements-traceability-matrix

title: RTM Traceability Attributes
content: RTM key attributes: REQ ID (unique identifier); Source (originating BR or SR); Priority (MoSCoW); Design Ref (design document section); Code Module (implementing file/component); Test Cases (TC IDs that verify); Status (lifecycle state); Owner (responsible BA/developer); Release (target release version).
tags: RTM attributes source priority design code test status owner release

title: Requirement Status Lifecycle
content: Status definitions: Draft (written not reviewed → submit for review); Under Review (being reviewed → complete review); Approved (stakeholder approved → begin design); Designed (design complete → begin implementation); In Progress (coding ongoing); Implemented (code complete → testing); Under Test (testing in progress); Verified (test passed); Rejected; Deferred; Pending.
tags: requirement-status lifecycle draft approved implemented verified deferred RTM

title: Traceability Chain End-to-End
content: Full chain: Business Goals → KPIs; Stakeholder Needs → Use Cases; Functional Requirements → Test Cases; Design Specs → API Specs; Code Modules → Unit Tests. Every artifact links to predecessors and successors. Break in chain = traceability gap = risk.
tags: traceability-chain end-to-end business-goals requirements design code tests

title: Blast Radius Analysis
content: Blast radius = scope of impact when a requirement changes. Analysis steps: 1.Identify changed requirement; 2.Trace backward (upstream impact on BR/SR); 3.Trace forward (downstream impact on design code tests docs); 4.Trace horizontal (peer requirements using same component/data); 5.Estimate effort; 6.Document and present to CCB.
tags: blast-radius impact-analysis change-management downstream upstream scope

title: Impact Analysis 6-Step Process
content: Impact analysis process: Step 1 Identify affected requirements (what is changing add/modify/delete); Step 2 Trace Backward (upstream business requirements stakeholder needs alignment check); Step 3 Trace Forward (design code test documentation affected); Step 4 Trace Horizontal (peer requirements dependencies interfaces); Step 5 Estimate effort and risk; Step 6 Document and present to CCB for decision.
tags: impact-analysis 6-steps backward forward horizontal effort CCB change

title: Impact Analysis Report Template
content: IAR sections: 1.Change Description (CR number requirement current/proposed state reason); 2.Affected Items (backward trace table; forward trace table with effort; horizontal trace table); 3.Effort Summary (requirements/design/dev/testing/documentation hours total); 4.Risk Assessment (likelihood/impact/mitigation); 5.Recommendation (Approve/Reject/Defer with rationale and approvals).
tags: impact-analysis-report template CCB effort risk recommendation approval

title: Change Control 5-Step Process
content: Change control: Step 1 Request (requestor submits CR; BA logs with unique ID; categorize type/priority; assign analyst; status→Under Analysis); Step 2 Analyze (clarify with requestor; perform impact analysis; estimate effort/cost; identify risks; document IAR; status→Assessed); Step 3 Review/Decide (present to CCB; discuss trade-offs; Approve/Reject/Defer; document rationale); Step 4 Implement (update requirements/design/code/RTM/tests); Step 5 Verify/Close (verify correct; regression test; update docs; notify requestor; close CR).
tags: change-control 5-steps CCB CR request analyze review implement verify

title: Change Request Form Fields
content: CR form fields: CR Number; Date; Requestor; Project; Release; Change Type (New/Modify/Delete/Defect/Enhancement/Clarification); Affected Requirement IDs; Current State description; Proposed Change description; Business Justification; Priority (Critical/High/Medium/Low); Requested Date; BA fields (Analyst assigned Date received Status Impact Analysis Ref).
tags: change-request CR form fields type priority justification status

title: Change Request Priority Levels
content: CR priority: Critical/Blocking (complete outage/security breach → respond immediately); High (major functionality impaired → fix this sprint); Medium (important improvement with workaround → plan next sprint); Low (minor enhancement cosmetic → backlog). Priority determines CCB meeting urgency and SLA for resolution.
tags: CR-priority critical high medium low blocking SLA urgency CCB

title: Requirements Baseline Definition
content: Baseline = formal approved snapshot of requirements at a point in time. Once baselined requirements are FROZEN; all changes require approved CR. Baseline rules: no changes without approved CR; version number incremented; previous baselines archived never deleted; all stakeholders notified.
tags: baseline definition frozen approved snapshot version change-control rules

title: Baseline Lifecycle States
content: Baseline lifecycle: Draft (working version editable) → Reviewed (review complete) → Approved (sign-off obtained) → Baselined (frozen read-only). Typical baselines: BL-1.0 initial after BRD approval; BL-1.1 after major change incorporated; BL-2.0 release 2.0 requirements. Archive all previous baselines.
tags: baseline lifecycle draft reviewed approved frozen version archive

title: Version Numbering Convention
content: Version numbering: Major scope change X.0→Y.0 (e.g. 1.0→2.0); Significant addition X.Y→X.Y+1 (1.0→1.1); Minor correction X.Y.Z→X.Y.Z+1 (1.0.0→1.0.1); Draft iteration X.Y-draft.N (1.0-draft.3). Apply consistently across requirements documents design specs and RTM.
tags: version-numbering convention major minor patch draft baseline requirements

title: Traceability Coverage Metrics
content: Coverage metrics: Backward Coverage = Reqs with Source / Total × 100% (target 100% red flag <95%); Forward Coverage = Reqs with Test / Total × 100% (target 100%); Orphan Rate = Orphans / Total × 100% (target 0% red flag >5%); Change Volume = CRs per Month (watch for increasing trend); CR Cycle Time target <5 days red flag >10 days; CR Approval Rate target 70-80%.
tags: traceability-metrics coverage backward forward orphan CR-cycle-time approval-rate

title: Orphan Requirements
content: Orphan requirement = requirement with no traceable source (no backward link to business need). Indicates: out-of-scope requirement; missing stakeholder need documentation; gold-plating. Action: validate with stakeholder if requirement is needed; if yes add source link; if no remove or defer. Orphan rate target = 0%.
tags: orphan-requirement no-source gold-plating backward-trace validation scope

title: Gold Plating Detection
content: Gold plating = code or design features without corresponding requirements. Detected by tracing forward: does every code module trace back to an approved requirement? If not: gold plating. Risks: unvalidated features; untested scope; maintenance burden; security vulnerabilities. Eliminate or formalize as requirements.
tags: gold-plating forward-trace unauthorized-feature scope-creep detection maintenance

title: Change Impact Assessment Categories
content: Impact categories when CR received: Requirements Impact (which requirements change); Design Impact (which design docs/components affected); Development Impact (which code modules need update); Testing Impact (which test cases need update or new TCs needed); Documentation Impact (user guides API docs); Deployment Impact (config schema migration needed).
tags: change-impact categories requirements design development testing documentation deployment

title: Change Control Board CCB
content: CCB (Change Control Board): decision-making body for requirement changes post-baseline. Members: BA Lead (facilitates); Tech Lead (technical impact); Product Owner (business decision); Project Manager (schedule cost impact); QA Lead (testing impact). Quorum required for approval. Document all decisions with rationale.
tags: CCB change-control-board members quorum decision approval governance

title: Traceability Tool Selection Matrix
content: Tool selection: Jira (agile integration ecosystem; Epic→Story→Subtask custom links); Azure DevOps (Microsoft stack CI/CD; work item linking query RTM); DOORS (complex regulatory aerospace defense medical; native traceability baselines); Confluence (documentation collaboration; labels links limited); Airtable/Notion (flexible small teams PoCs); Excel (universal simple projects manual).
tags: traceability-tools Jira Azure-DevOps DOORS Confluence Excel selection

title: Jira Traceability Configuration
content: Jira hierarchy: Business Requirement (BR-xxx) → Epic (EPIC-xxx) → User Story (US-xxx) → Task (TASK-xxx)/Bug (BUG-xxx); Test Case (TC-xxx via Zephyr/Xray). Link types: "implements/is implemented by" (FR→Code); "tests/is tested by" (FR→TC); "derives from/is parent of" (BR→FR); "depends on/blocks" (FR→FR). RTM via JQL export.
tags: Jira traceability configuration hierarchy link-types RTM Zephyr Xray setup

title: Azure DevOps Traceability Setup
content: Azure DevOps work item hierarchy: Feature (Business Req) → Product Backlog Item/User Story (Functional Req) → Task (Implementation) + Test Case (Verification) + Bug (Defect). Link types: Parent/Child (hierarchical); Tests/Tested By; Related. RTM via WIQL query: SELECT from WorkItemLinks WHERE Source.WorkItemType=Feature MODE(Recursive).
tags: Azure-DevOps traceability work-items hierarchy WIQL RTM configuration

title: Traceability Setup Checklist
content: RTM setup: RTM created with standard columns; all requirements have unique IDs; traceability approach defined (tool manual hybrid); link types established; status definitions documented. Maintenance: all requirements trace backward to source; all trace forward to design/test; no orphans; no untested requirements; no gold plating; RTM updated when requirements change; regular health checks.
tags: traceability-checklist setup maintenance orphan gold-plating coverage health-check

title: Change Management Checklist
content: Change management checklist: Change control process documented; CR form and workflow established; CCB members identified; impact analysis template available; all changes go through CR process after baseline; decisions documented with rationale. Baseline management: criteria defined; version numbering scheme established; archive maintained; stakeholders notified of new baselines.
tags: change-management checklist CR CCB baseline archive notification governance

title: Requirement Traceability Attributes Complete
content: Full set of RTM attributes for enterprise use: ID; Title; Type (FR/NFR/BR); Priority; Status; Source (BR/SR ID); Design Ref; Code Ref; Test Case IDs; Release; Owner; Rationale; Verification Method; Baseline Version; Last Modified Date; Change History. Rationale attribute documents WHY requirement exists.
tags: RTM attributes complete ID source design code test rationale baseline version owner

title: Traceability Automation Considerations
content: Automation options: Jira/ADO auto-link on commit message (e.g. "Implements FR-001"); test automation framework tags requirements (pytest markers); CI/CD pipeline generates traceability report; DOORS/Polarion provide native traceability engines. Manual RTM maintenance error-prone at scale; invest in tooling when >100 requirements. Hybrid approach: tool for links; Excel for reporting.
tags: traceability-automation Jira commit-message CI-CD requirements-tag tooling scale

title: Requirements Coverage Gap Analysis
content: Coverage gap analysis: identify requirements with missing forward links (no design or no test); identify requirements with status Pending/Draft past baseline; identify test cases not linked to any requirement (orphan tests). Report as % metrics. Present gaps at sprint review or CCB. Target: 100% backward and forward coverage before release.
tags: coverage-gap analysis requirements test missing forward-link orphan test metrics

title: Traceability Health Check Process
content: Regular traceability health check: monthly or per sprint. Steps: 1.Run orphan report (reqs without source); 2.Run untested report (reqs without TCs); 3.Run gold-plating report (code without reqs); 4.Check status distribution (too many Pending = bottleneck); 5.Review CR cycle time trend; 6.Update stale RTM entries. Output: health dashboard with red/amber/green status.
tags: traceability health-check monthly orphan untested gold-plating status dashboard

title: Requirements Baseline Change Protocol
content: After baseline established: all proposed changes require formal CR; no informal changes to baselined documents; BA maintains change log; version number updates on every CR approval; notify all stakeholders of new baseline; update RTM to reflect new baseline version; previous baselines accessible in archive. Violations = configuration management failure.
tags: baseline-change-protocol CR formal-change version-log notification archive CM

title: Traceability in Regulated Industries
content: Regulatory traceability requirements: FDA 21 CFR Part 11 (software validation; bidirectional traceability mandatory); IEC 62304 (medical device software; traceability from safety requirements to tests); DO-178C (avionics; every requirement traced to test evidence); ISO 26262 (automotive safety; requirements to safety goals). Non-compliance = audit finding or product recall.
tags: regulated-industries traceability FDA IEC62304 DO-178C ISO26262 compliance audit

title: CR Cycle Time Metric
content: CR Cycle Time = days from CR submission to closure. Target: <5 days for High priority; <10 days for Medium; <20 days for Low. Red flag: >10 days average indicates CCB bottleneck or insufficient BA capacity. Track monthly trend. Increasing cycle time = degrading change management process. Root causes: insufficient CCB frequency; complex impact analyses; unclear ownership.
tags: CR-cycle-time metric target red-flag trend bottleneck measurement governance
